title: Electric Shock/Electrocution
immediate_action: Do NOT touch the person if still in contact with the source. Disconnect power first.

steps.instruction: STOP. Do NOT touch the person if they are still in contact with the electrical source. Turn off the power at the main switch/breaker.
steps.tip: Electricity can travel through you. Use a dry non-conducting object (wooden broom, rubber mat) to separate them from the source.

steps.instruction: Call emergency services immediately. Mention electrical injury — they may need specialized cardiac monitoring.
steps.tip: Even if the person seems fine, internal injuries and cardiac arrhythmias can develop hours later.

steps.instruction: Check for breathing and pulse. If not breathing, begin CPR immediately.
steps.tip: Electrical shock can cause cardiac arrest. Early CPR is critical.

steps.instruction: Look for entry AND exit burns. Electrical burns may look minor on the skin but cause massive internal damage.
steps.tip: Cover burns with sterile dressing. Do NOT apply water or ointments.

steps.instruction: Treat for shock: keep warm, elevate legs if no spinal injury suspected, monitor consciousness.
steps.tip: Do NOT move them if fall from height is possible — suspect spinal injury.

do_not: Do NOT touch person if source is still active
Do NOT use metal or wet objects to separate from source
Do NOT assume they're okay — internal damage may not be visible
Do NOT use water on electrical burns while near live electricity
